page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 11 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance characteristics, particularly in memory-intensive and single-core operations. The instance achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing tests. Memory bandwidth read-only benchmarks for both cached and uncached blocks rank in the top 25 percent, supported by the high-speed DDR5 memory architecture. CPU-bound workloads benefit from advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions, which drive strong performance in cryptography, database operations, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference. However, multi-core stress-ng performance and large-scale LLM text generation fall into the average category, highlighting a relative tradeoff when executing highly parallelized compute-bound tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i-flex.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density for memory-heavy applications without the premium cost of local NVMe storage. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong database operation benchmarks make this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads. Recommended use cases include relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caching layers such as Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate-scale machine learning inference tasks that leverage CPU-based matrix acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 384.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, resulting in an 8.0 GB per core allocation. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides an 11 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core, image, and text processing capabilities, alongside strong memory read bandwidth and database performance. It also supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, making it highly efficient for vector and matrix operations. This server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including database management, in-memory caching, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for database operations and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel matrix extensions for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector calculations
11 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Top-tier performance in single-core execution and memory read bandwidth
Best suited for databases, in-memory caching, and CPU-based inference
